expectations.text: Response presents 2 or more distinct named strategy options before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Two named options are presented: 'Option A — Parallel (Expand-Contract) with Implementation-first Bridge' (marked as recommended) and 'Option B — Branch by Abstraction (new interface + dual implementation)'. Both appear in Step 6 before any Phase 2 work begins.

expectations.text: Each strategy option includes explicit effort and risk ratings
expectations.evidence: Option A states 'Effort: Medium' and 'Risk: Low'. Option B states 'Effort: Medium-High' and 'Risk: Low-Medium'. Both options include explicit labeled ratings.

expectations.text: Branch by Abstraction is mentioned as a viable option given the stable public interface
expectations.evidence: Option B is explicitly titled 'Branch by Abstraction (new interface + dual implementation)' and is described as 'Viable, but adds unnecessary complexity for this case.' It is offered as a real alternative, not dismissed. The response also notes 'Offer this if the team prefers explicit interface versioning over a compat bridge file.'

expectations.text: Agent stops and waits for user to choose a strategy before proceeding to Phase 2
expectations.evidence: The response ends with a 'Waiting for User Choice' section that explicitly states 'Phase 2 (Snapshot — writing characterization tests) will not begin until a strategy is chosen and these questions are answered.' The document contains no Phase 2 content.

expectations.text: Absence of existing unit tests is identified as a factor affecting snapshot cost
expectations.evidence: Under 'Test Coverage' in Step 5: 'No existing unit tests. This is the highest-risk factor in this migration. There is no behavioral safety net. The Snapshot phase (Phase 2) must create characterization tests from scratch before any production code is touched.' Also echoed in the strategy context table: 'Test coverage: Zero — Characterization tests must be written before any code changes.'

expectations.text: Agent identifies RxJava-specific library integrations (e.g., room-rxjava2, retrofit adapter-rxjava2, rxandroid) as dependencies that need to be swapped out as part of the migration
expectations.evidence: Step 5b contains a dependency table explicitly listing: 'io.reactivex.rxjava2:rxandroid' (action: Replace), 'com.squareup.retrofit2:adapter-rxjava2' (action: Remove), and 'androidx.room:room-rxjava2' (action: Replace with room-ktx). All three are identified with concrete replacement/removal actions.
